title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye bags

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye bags because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work to combat puffiness and dark circles. The capsules' active ingredients, including centella asiatica and ascorbic acid, help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation around the delicate orbital area. As a result, the appearance of eye bags is significantly reduced, leaving eyes looking brighter and more youthful. Additionally, maidenhair tree's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the skin, providing long-lasting relief from puffiness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: The capsules contain adaptogens like Gynostemma pentaphyllum, which helps to reduce stress and fatigue by normalizing cortisol levels, thus alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Maidenhair Tree extract, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to eye bags and dark circles.

Improvement of blood circulation: The herbal capsules contain compounds that improve blood circulation and reduce blood stagnation, which can help to alleviate puffiness and discoloration under the eyes by promoting healthy blood flow.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: It helps reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and improving lymphatic drainage.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid antioxidant, it reduces oxidative stress and inflammation in the eye area, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
Ginkgolide B: It has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and fluid retention in the orbital area, leading to a reduction in eye bag appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
Seeds: Ginkgo seed extract is also used in eye care products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce eye strain and promote overall eye health.
Barks: Some eye care products may contain Ginkgo biloba bark extract, which is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Obtain 500mg of ginkgo biloba extract standardized to 24% flavone glycosides from a reputable supplier within 2 days.
Grind 100 capsules of gelatin into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Weigh 500mg of the ginkgo biloba extract using a digital scale to achieve accurate dosages in 5 minutes.
Fill the ground gelatin capsules with the weighed ginkgo biloba extract using a vacuum sealer and machine in 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory capsules helps with eye bags because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and dark circles. The capsules also help to improve blood circulation, which can reduce the appearance of bags under the eyes. Additionally, chicory's natural astringent properties can help to tighten the skin and reduce the look of fine lines and wrinkles, leaving you with a more radiant and refreshed complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, alleviating dark circles and eye bags.
Antioxidant properties: Chicory's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to skin cells, leading to discoloration and puffiness around the eyes.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Chicory's vasoconstrictors help reduce blood flow to the under-eye area, minimizing fluid retention and reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin helps reduce fluid retention and swelling in the face, which can contribute to eye bags. It acts as a natural diuretic and helps remove excess fluids from the body.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a type of phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. It also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes.

Kaempferol: Kaempferol, a flavonoid compound, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. It also has vasoprotective properties that can help improve blood circulation and reduce fluid accumulation in the face.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for eye bags due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the eyes.
Seeds: Used for eye bags because they contain saponins, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
Roots: Used for eye bags due to their potential to act as a natural diuretic, which can help reduce water retention and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried roots of cichorium intybus on a sunny day to ensure potency.
Dry the harvested roots further in a dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powder with 200g of gelatin and 100g of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 500 empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and dark circles. The herbal extract soothes and calms the skin under the eyes, reducing swelling and discoloration. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to bagging and fatigue around the eyes. By taking chamomile capsules regularly, you can expect a noticeable reduction in the appearance of eye bags, leaving your eyes looking brighter and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating eye bag discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to skin and underlying tissues, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile's sedative compounds help relax the muscles around the eyes, reduce puffiness, and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to get a good night's sleep (which can also help alleviate eye bag issues).
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and puffiness associated with eye bags.
Luteolin: This flavonoid exhibits antioxidant properties, protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and promoting overall skin health.

Matricaria chamomilla essential oil (including alpha-bisabolol and bisabolol oxide): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, calming the skin and reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to reduce puffiness and alleviate eye strain.
Seeds: Utilized for their calming and sedative effects to promote relaxation and reduce eye fatigue.
Leaves: Employed for their antioxidant properties to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day with low humidity.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags for 2-3 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 1 gram of gelatin powder and 1 gram of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry capsules helps with eye bags because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain extracts from the highbush cranberry fruit, which has been traditionally used to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. The antioxidants in highbush cranberry help to combat free radicals that can cause damage to delicate skin around the eyes, while its anti-inflammatory properties constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, resulting in a more radiant and refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Highbush cranberry capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Vasoprotective Effects: The bioactive compounds in highbush cranberry capsules have been shown to improve blood vessel elasticity and strength, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, including those that can make eye bags look more prominent.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Highbush cranberry capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to puffy or baggy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes associated with eye bags.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, quercetin helps reduce swelling and alleviate eye strain, contributing to a more refreshed appearance.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Used due to their high content of anthocyanins, which may have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for eye health.
Leaves: Possibly used for their antioxidant properties, which may help reduce puffiness and inflammation associated with eye bags.
Seeds: May be utilized for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could aid in reducing the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries, ensure they are free from contamination and debris.
Grind 50g of the dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered vaccinium macrocarpon with 50g of vegetable cellulose powder and mix well.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered vaccinium macrocarpon mixture and seal the capsules.
Label and store the vaccinium macrocarpon capsules in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with eye bags because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and reduce puffiness. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and green tea can help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. By taking these capsules, you can enjoy a more refreshed and rejuvenated look, without the need for harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Caffeine-free Peppermint: Helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels, reducing fluid retention, and promoting lymphatic drainage.
Aloe Vera: Soothes and calms the skin under the eyes, reducing redness and inflammation, while also providing antioxidant benefits to combat signs of aging.
Vitamin K-rich Green Tea: Enhances circulation, reduces swelling, and helps to reduce the appearance of dark circles by promoting healthy blood vessel function.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These flavonoid compounds help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.
Theaflavins: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate the appearance of eye bags.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness under the eyes, making it an effective remedy for eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants, particularly catechins, which help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
Buds: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Stems: Employed for their potential to reduce puffiness and promote lymphatic drainage, which can help alleviate eye bag issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of camellia sinensis leaves by cutting them at the base of the stem for optimal freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius to remove moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powdered camellia sinensis with 8 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with eye bags because they contain potent herbs that reduce puffiness and dark circles. The antioxidants present in these capsules combat free radicals, which can cause inflammation and swelling around the eyes. Additionally, the herbal extracts help to improve circulation, reducing the appearance of blue or purple discoloration under the eyes. By targeting both the root causes of eye bags, such as fluid retention and poor circulation, lime capsules provide a natural and effective solution for a brighter, more refreshed look.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The herbal extracts in these capsules, such as ginkgo biloba and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate eye bag discomfort.
Vasoconstrictive Properties: Certain herbs like bitter melon and licorice root can help to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup under the eyes and alleviating dark circles.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can contribute to a more rested and reduced appearance of eye bags.
Terpenes: Specifically, terpenes like borneol and bornyl acetate have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, such as chlorogenic and caffeic acids, have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, leading to a decrease in eye bag appearance.
remedy_parts: Buds: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
Leaves: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help calm and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried tilia europaea flowers from a reliable source at the peak of potency.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1g of the tilia europaea powder with 2g of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with eye bags because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that work wonders in reducing puffiness and dark circles. The extract of Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, has natural decongestant and soothing effects that help to calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing swelling and discoloration. As a result, the eye area appears smoother and more radiant, giving you a brighter and more youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles and puffiness under the eyes.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The herbal capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and rosmarinic acid, which may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the orbital fat pads that can cause eye bags.

Vasoconstrictive Effects: Purple coneflower's flavonoids, such as quercetin, have vasoconstrictive properties, which can help narrow blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside, help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness associated with eye bags by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Polyphenols: Polyphenols, such as caffeic acid and chicoric acid, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help minimize eye bag appearance by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin.

Triterpenoid saponins: Triterpenoid saponins, like echinacynic acid, have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate eye bags by reducing swelling, inflammation, and fluid retention in the facial area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and polysaccharides that help reduce inflammation and improve eye health.
Flowers: Contain echinacoside and alkylamides that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate eye bag symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers from a reputable source.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40c for 2 hours.
Grind 20g of dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered echinacea purpurea with 80g of gelatin capsules and 10g of silica powder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400mg of powdered echinacea purpurea mixture using a capsule filler machine.

The antioxidants in dandelion extract, such as vitamins A and K, work to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation around the eyes, while its anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye area, contributing to the alleviation of eye bags.
Taraxasterol: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Saponins: These compounds have a diuretic effect, which can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate eye bags caused by water accumulation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron.

Seeds: The seeds are used for their oil content, which is believed to have antioxidant properties and may help reduce swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 taraxacum officinale roots, clean them thoroughly and dry them in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 0.5 grams of the powder with 0.5 grams of gelatin and 2.5 milliliters of water to create a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the paste, using 0.5 grams per capsule and 100 capsules in total.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with eye bags because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and swelling. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of histamine, a chemical that can cause blood vessels to dilate and lead to fluid accumulation under the eyes. By reducing inflammation and fluid retention, St John's Wort capsules may help to alleviate eye bags and leave you looking more rested and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: St John's Wort's adaptogenic properties help reduce inflammation and stress response in the body, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in St John's Wort capsules can neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress, which can lead to dark circles and discoloration under the eyes.

Vasoconstrictive Properties: St John's Wort's vasoconstrictive properties help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and puffiness under the eyes by minimizing fluid accumulation in the delicate tissues around the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A terpene derivative that may help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags by inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators and improving blood circulation.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Rutin): A group of phenolic compounds that possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate eye bag symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin.

Hypericin: A naphthodianthrone that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to reducing eye bag swelling and discoloration by modulating inflammatory responses and protecting against oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high flavonoid content, which may help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.

Seeds: The seeds are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce swelling and promote healthy skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the summer months when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum plant material in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum plant material into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 1 part of the hypericum perforatum powder with 2 parts of vegetable cellulose and a small amount of water to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with eye bags because they contain allantoin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces puffiness and swelling. The capsules also promote healthy skin cell regeneration, which can help to reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. Additionally, comfrey has natural antioxidant properties that protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant.

remedy_constituents: Allantoin: A glycosaminoglycan that helps reduce inflammation and promotes the breakdown of edema, which contributes to eye bags.
Triflavin (a flavonoid): A potent antioxidant that helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Mucilages (complex polysaccharides): Soothing and hydrating properties that help reduce puffiness and inflammation, promoting a smoother and more even skin tone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for eye bags due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Roots: Used to make capsules for eye bags due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the skin around the eyes.
Buds: Used to make capsules for eye bags due to their astringent properties that help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh symphytum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at a temperature of 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered plant material with 1 gram of microcrystalline cellulose and 0.5 gram of silicon dioxide.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eye bags because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that target the underlying causes of puffiness. The aloe vera gel inside the capsules penetrates deep into the skin, reducing swelling and inflammation around the delicate orbital bone area. As a result, it diminishes dark circles, reduces puffiness, and leaves the under-eye area looking smoother, brighter, and more radiant. With regular use, herbal aloe capsules can help you wake up to fresh, well-rested-looking eyes every morning.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in herbal aloe capsules, like vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of dark circles and puffy eyes.

Moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing abilities help to hydrate and soothe dry, tired eyes and surrounding skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags and leaving the area feeling soft and supple.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Aloe rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and puffiness associated with eye bags.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried aloe vera powder and grind it into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with the ground aloe vera paste in a glass bowl until a smooth consistency is achieved after 5 minutes.
Steep one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers in the aloe vera mixture for 20 minutes to infuse the herbal remedy.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the infused herbal solution and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal Beach Rose Capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, alleviating eye bags.
Antioxidant property: The capsules' antioxidants neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to delicate skin around the eyes, reducing dark circles and discoloration.
Vasodilatory property: Herbal Beach Rose Capsules help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid retention under the eyes, which can contribute to eye bags.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles around the eyes.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, alleviating eye bag swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa rugosa petals and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Dry the petals in a warm, dry place for 24 hours or until they are crumbly and brittle.
Grind the dried petals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the rosa rugosa powder with 500mg of vitamin c and fill capsules using an encapsulation machine.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Canada thistle capsules helps with eye bags because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and discoloration. Barks: Contains flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cirsium arvense root extract from a trusted supplier and store it in an airtight container.
Grind the cirsium arvense root extract into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the ground cirsium arvense powder with 250mg of gelatin capsules in a small bowl, and stir for 2 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and fill each capsule with approximately 750mcg of herbal content within 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with eye bags because they promote relaxation and reduce puffiness. The root of the valerian plant is rich in antioxidants, which help to combat oxidative stress that can contribute to dark circles and swollen eyes. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow to the delicate skin around the eyes. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Valerian root has adaptogenic properties, which means it helps your body respond to stress and promotes relaxation, reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Sedative and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The sedative compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerinol, can help reduce inflammation and promote a sense of calmness, which can alleviate eye bag issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and chop them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the chopped valeriana roots in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours or until they become brittle.
Grind the dried valeriana roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and sift through a fine-mesh strainer.
Mix one part of the valeriana root powder with two parts of silica gel in a bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Fill empty capsules with the infused herb mixture, approximately 250mg per capsule, and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with eye bags because they promote healthy blood circulation, which reduces puffiness and dark circles. Buds: Contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties, soothing and calming the skin around the eyes to alleviate fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in morning for optimal oil content and potency.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using shade drying or air circulation method.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into fine powder using coffee grinder or mortar pestle.
Mix 400mg of powdered peppermint with 100mg of vegetable filler such as silica gel in small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with prepared mixture and store in airtight container for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort capsules helps with eye bags because it effectively reduces puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried bupleurum falcatum root bark and two parts dried peppermint leaves by weight.
Grind the combined herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herbal blend with 20% cornstarch to prevent clumping and improve flowability for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 400mg of the herbal blend mixture and seal tightly within 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with eye bags because it effectively reduces inflammation and swelling in the face and eyes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in stinging nettle, such as flavonoids and beta-carotene, help to combat puffiness and dark circles by reducing fluid retention and improving blood circulation. Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, soothing and protecting the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh nettle leaves and stems in the morning when dew is still present.
Rinse the harvested material thoroughly with filtered water and drain well for 30 minutes.
Dry the rinsed nettles using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours or air drying for 24 hours.
Blend 1 cup of dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes, then sift through a mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powdered nettle and seal immediately for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with eye bags because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces puffiness and darkness under the eyes. Roots: Turmeric roots contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help improve circulation and reduce water retention, alleviating eye bag discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500mg of dried turmeric powder into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 1000mg of glycerin and 100mg of vitamin e oil in a small bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the turmeric-glycerin-vitamin e mixture using a capsule filler machine for 10 seconds per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years or refrigerate for longer storage.
Consume one to two capsules daily before bedtime for at least 6 weeks to notice visible reduction in eye bags and puffiness.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye bags are natural supplements designed to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. They help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with eye bags by promoting healthy circulation, reducing inflammation, and soothing the skin. The best herbal capsules for eye bags include Ginkgo biloba, Aloe barbadensis, Camellia sinensis, Ruscus aculeatus, Vitis vinifera, Centella asiatica, Silybum marianum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Euphrasia officinalis, and Avena sativa. If left untreated, eye bags can lead to a tired and aged appearance, impacting one's confidence and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal capsules are dark circles, puffiness, and fine lines around the eyes. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like bilberry, grape seed extract, and peptides help to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote collagen production, resulting in a smoother, more radiant appearance. These remedies can also alleviate symptoms of sinus pressure, migraines, and allergies that can contribute to eye bag-like conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye bags is a blend of Caffeine, Peppermint, and Ginkgo Biloba. Caffeine reduces puffiness by constricting blood vessels, while Peppermint cools and calms the skin, reducing inflammation. Ginkgo Biloba improves circulation and reduces swelling. Additionally, supplements like Rosehip and Aloe Vera can also help to nourish and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of eye bags. This combination can be taken in capsule form, typically 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate eye bag symptoms.
